few_shot_examples.title: [eprh] Add back a no-op for removed component-hook-factories rule
few_shot_examples.body:
The `component-hook-factories` rule was removed in #35825 as part of a feature flag cleanup, but was listed in the README as part of the manual config example. This broke users who used a manual config (copied from the old README) in eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.0. This adds back a deprecated no-op rule as a fix.


#35825 removed other rules (`automatic-effect-dependencies` and `fire`), but these were for experimental features that did not ship. These were also not referenced in the README.\

few_shot_examples.title: Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test
few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

few_shot_examples.title: [eprh] Add back a no-op for removed component-hook-factories rule
few_shot_examples.body:
The `component-hook-factories` rule was removed in #35825 as part of a feature flag cleanup, but was listed in the README as part of the manual config example. This broke users who used a manual config (copied from the old README) in eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.0. This adds back a deprecated no-op rule as a fix.


#35825 removed other rules (`automatic-effect-dependencies` and `fire`), but these were for experimental features that did not ship. These were also not referenced in the README.\

few_shot_examples.title: Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test
few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

few_shot_examples.title: [eprh] Add back a no-op for removed component-hook-factories rule
few_shot_examples.body:
The `component-hook-factories` rule was removed in #35825 as part of a feature flag cleanup, but was listed in the README as part of the manual config example. This broke users who used a manual config (copied from the old README) in eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.0. This adds back a deprecated no-op rule as a fix.


#35825 removed other rules (`automatic-effect-dependencies` and `fire`), but these were for experimental features that did not ship. These were also not referenced in the README.\

few_shot_examples.title: Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test
few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

The `component-hook-factories` rule was removed in #35825 as part of a feature flag cleanup, but was listed in the README as part of the manual config example. This broke users who used a manual config (copied from the old README) in eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.0. This adds back a deprecated no-op rule as a fix.


#35825 removed other rules (`automatic-effect-dependencies` and `fire`), but these were for experimental features that did not ship. These were also not referenced in the README.\

few_shot_examples.title: Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test
few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

few_shot_examples.title: [eprh] Add back a no-op for removed component-hook-factories rule
few_shot_examples.body:
The `component-hook-factories` rule was removed in #35825 as part of a feature flag cleanup, but was listed in the README as part of the manual config example. This broke users who used a manual config (copied from the old README) in eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.0. This adds back a deprecated no-op rule as a fix.


#35825 removed other rules (`automatic-effect-dependencies` and `fire`), but these were for experimental features that did not ship. These were also not referenced in the README.\

few_shot_examples.title: Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test
few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

This broke users who used a manual config (copied from the old README) in eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.0. This adds back a deprecated no-op rule as a fix.


#35825 removed other rules (`automatic-effect-dependencies` and `fire`), but these were for experimental features that did not ship. These were also not referenced in the README.\

few_shot_examples.title: Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test
few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

few_shot_examples.title: [eprh] Add back a no-op for removed component-hook-factories rule
few_shot_examples.body:
The `component-hook-factories` rule was removed in #35825 as part of a feature flag cleanup, but was listed in the README as part of the manual config example. This broke users who used a manual config (copied from the old README) in eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.0. This adds back a deprecated no-op rule as a fix.


#35825 removed other rules (`automatic-effect-dependencies` and `fire`), but these were for experimental features that did not ship. These were also not referenced in the README.\

few_shot_examples.title: Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test
few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

few_shot_examples.title: [eprh] Add back a no-op for removed component-hook-factories rule
few_shot_examples.body:
The `component-hook-factories` rule was removed in #35825 as part of a feature flag cleanup, but was listed in the README as part of the manual config example. This broke users who used a manual config (copied from the old README) in eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.0. This adds back a deprecated no-op rule as a fix.


#35825 removed other rules (`automatic-effect-dependencies` and `fire`), but these were for experimental features that did not ship. These were also not referenced in the README.\

few_shot_examples.title: Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test
few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

few_shot_examples.title: [eprh] Add back a no-op for removed component-hook-factories rule
few_shot_examples.body:
The `component-hook-factories` rule was removed in #35825 as part of a feature flag cleanup, but was listed in the README as part of the manual config example. This broke users who used a manual config (copied from the old README) in eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.0. This adds back a deprecated no-op rule as a fix.


#35825 removed other rules (`automatic-effect-dependencies` and `fire`), but these were for experimental features that did not ship. These were also not referenced in the README.\

few_shot_examples.title: Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test
few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

few_shot_examples.title: [eprh] Add back a no-op for removed component-hook-factories rule
few_shot_examples.body:
The `component-hook-factories` rule was removed in #35825 as part of a feature flag cleanup, but was listed in the README as part of the manual config example. This broke users who used a manual config (copied from the old README) in eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.0. This adds back a deprecated no-op rule as a fix.


#35825 removed other rules (`automatic-effect-dependencies` and `fire`), but these were for experimental features that did not ship. These were also not referenced in the README.\

few_shot_examples.title: Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test
few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

The `component-hook-factories` rule was removed in #35825 as part of a feature flag cleanup, but was listed in the README as part of the manual config example. This broke users who used a manual config (copied from the old README) in eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.0. This adds back a deprecated no-op rule as a fix.


#35825 removed other rules (`automatic-effect-dependencies` and `fire`), but these were for experimental features that did not ship. These were also not referenced in the README.\

few_shot_examples.title: Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test
few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`

few_shot_examples.title: [eprh] Add back a no-op for removed component-hook-factories rule
few_shot_examples.body:
The `component-hook-factories` rule was removed in #35825 as part of a feature flag cleanup, but was listed in the README as part of the manual config example. This broke users who used a manual config (copied from the old README) in eslint-plugin-react-hooks 7.1.0. This adds back a deprecated no-op rule as a fix.


#35825 removed other rules (`automatic-effect-dependencies` and `fire`), but these were for experimental features that did not ship. These were also not referenced in the README.\

few_shot_examples.title: Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test
few_shot_examples.body:
## Summary

Fixes #134565

As discussed in the issue, the `TestSyncHandler` test in `pkg/controller/volume/ephemeral/controller_test.go` leaks goroutines because it uses `context.Background()` which never cancels, leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.

Now that context support has been added to `tools/cache` (#126387), the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation. This PR:

- Replaces `context.Background()` with `context.WithCancel()`
